Fixture: tiny
Purpose: smallest viable backup; happy-path e2e exercise.
File count: 3
Total bytes: 20
SHA256-of-tree: 44511b29c1650c20d661ffabd97517b18ce2b35da630e9ee76e5eb76b1a11d19

SHA256-of-tree recipe (must match the Go implementation in
test/e2e/helpers.go and the shell version in regen-manifest.sh):

  1. List every regular file under the fixture dir, relative paths.
  2. Sort the list lexicographically by raw bytes (LC_ALL=C).
  3. For each path: write the bytes of the relative path, one newline
     (0x0a), the file contents, one newline (0x0a), into a hash buffer.
  4. SHA-256 the buffer. Hex-encode.

The framing newline after each chunk prevents the concat-collision
attack where files A="x" + B="y" and A="xy" + B="" would share a hash.

File inventory:

  a.txt      4 bytes   "aaa\n"
  b.md       6 bytes   "hello\n"
  c.json    10 bytes   '{"k":"v"}\n'

ACs that consume this fixture:
  AC-1  init APFS happy path  (used as the source tree under the
                               init test's backup)
  AC-3  happy-path copy
  AC-9  verify intact backup
  AC-11 concurrency lock (second instance refused)
  AC-15 non-TTY fallback (small enough that backup completes
        before the test's deadline regardless of the rsync flavour)

Stability:
  - Files committed to git in static form.
  - SHA256-of-tree must remain stable across machines; do not edit
    these files without re-running test/fixtures/regen-manifest.sh
    and updating this line.
